items.title: Carrick bespreekt 'kappersfinale': 'Weet wie het is, maar hij heeft geen invloed'
items.summary: Manchester United speelt dit seizoen geen Europees voetbal en lag ook al heel snel uit de beide Engelse bekertoernooien. Toch spelen de Red Devils een finale, voor één inmiddels tamelijk harige supporter althans. Manager Michael Carrick erkende daags voor het duel met West Ham United dat hij weet wie deze Frank Ilett is.

items.title: Cazorla: 'LaLiga light years away from the PL'
items.summary: Santi Cazorla has taken a swipe at LaLiga after his team's league game at Rayo Vallecano on Saturday afternoon was postponed just hours before kickoff.

items.title: Van Dijk valt in herhaling na zoveelste late opdoffer voor Liverpool
items.summary: Liverpool slikte zondag voor de achtste keer dit seizoen een bepalende tegentreffer na de tachtigste minuut van een Premier League-wedstrijd: tegen Manchester City gaven The Reds de voorsprong zelfs helemaal uit handen. Na de nederlaag viel aanvoerder Virgil van Dijk in herhaling.

items.title: Verhuurde Duijvestijn bespreekt ongelukkige start bij Sparta
items.summary: Lance Duijvestijn scoorde zaterdag namens Fortuna Sittard tegen zijn broodheer Sparta Rotterdam. De aanvallende middenvelder heeft nu al twee keer gescoord in Limburgse dienst, terwijl hij in Rotterdam-West op nul competitiedoelpunten bleef staan. Na afloop van de wedstrijd stond hij Voetbal International te woord.

items.title: De Vinicius Jr. van de lage landen: 'Hij solliciteert naar een topclub'
items.summary: In de rubriek Nederlander van de Week kiest buitenlands voetbalkenner Süleyman Öztürk elke week een speler die hem het meest kon bekoren in de afgelopen speelronde in competities buiten Nederland. Opnieuw is de keuze gevallen op Crysencio Summerville.

items.title: FIA want F1 engine controversy solved 'not in the courtroom'
items.summary: The FIA say they want to keep a dispute between Formula 1 teams over the sport's new 2026 power units out of "the courtroom" as they seek to solve the issue before the start of the new season.

items.title: Laporta resigns as Barça president, will re-stand
items.summary: Joan Laporta has resigned as Barcelona president ahead of re-standing for the role when club members go to the polls on March 15.

items.title: Late goals are destroying Liverpool's season and the answers aren't obvious
items.summary: Why have Liverpool gone from mentality monsters to a side there for the taking?

items.title: Argentijnse bond grijpt in en wil nieuwe Messi-scenario's voorkomen
items.summary: De Argentijnse voetbalbond heeft een opvallende maatregel ingevoerd die jonge talenten moet ontmoedigen om vroeg naar het buitenland te vertrekken. Wie niet eerst een profcontract tekent bij een Argentijnse club, komt voortaan niet meer in aanmerking voor een uitnodiging voor een nationale ploeg.

items.title: 'Hell yeah!' - Kubiak confirms Raiders head coaching move after Super Bowl
items.summary: Seattle Seahawks offensive coordinator Klint Kubiak has essentially confirmed he will be the next Las Vegas Raiders head coach following his side's Super Bowl 60 win over the New England Patriots.

items.title: Barça on Super League exit: No benefit to stay
items.summary: Barcelona president Joan Laporta said the club quit the European Super League to "rejoin the football family."

items.title: Haaland earns City dramatic late win at Liverpool
items.summary: Erling Haaland kept Manchester City in the title race with an added-time penalty as Pep Guardiola's side came from behind to win 2-1 in a chaotic finish.

items.title: 'You have to feel the waves of emotion' - Carrick on 'buzz' of winning
items.summary: Manchester United head coach Michael Carrick says it would be "boring and pointless" if his players did not celebrate victories.

items.title: St Pauli plotting their next miracle in tantalising Bundesliga survival battle | Andy Brassell
items.summary: Win against Stuttgart was a reminder that unity remains St Pauli’s greatest strength in defying the odds again It had begun to look like a lost cause. In a season where the Bundesliga’s relegation battle increasingly promises a richness that the title race may lack (with all due respect to Borussia Dortmund’s efforts to stalk Bayern Munich at closer quarters in recent weeks), it has felt like St Pauli were, like fellow minnows Heidenheim, ready to be cut away. The Hamburg club’s best-ever start to a top-flight season, two wins and a draw from their first three games, felt like an age ago. Nine successive defeats will do that to you. Yet these masters of the unusual and the unexpected had another surprise up their sleeve this weekend; not least, one suspects, to themselves. Stuttgart travelled north on a fine run of form, sitting pretty in a Champions League spot and fresh from a week of qualifying for the DFB Pokal semi-finals, a trophy which they have every hope of retaining. With one league win against largely hopeless Heidenheim since that golden start for their hosts, who are also harbouring an injury list as long as one of Scottie Pippen’s arms (to paraphrase Jay-Z), it looked straightforward for Sebastian Hoeness and his men. Continue reading...

items.title: VAR calls leave De Rossi and Spalletti fuming as Napoli prevail at the last | Nicky Bandini
items.summary: VAR’s application has been a divisive topic everywhere it has been introduced. It was more of the same in Serie A You might not be shocked to learn that Daniele De Rossi thinks football has gone soft. Since retiring and moving into management, the man with the “beware the sliding tackle” tattoo has acknowledged he sometimes misses getting to stick the boot in. But would the stick figure seen flying into an opponent on the back of his right calf even stand a chance in this era of VAR? “I don’t know what to say any more,” lamented De Rossi after his Genoa team lost 3-2 to Napoli on Saturday. “The football we played no longer exists. We were naïve, but it seems I don’t know anything. I don’t know what sport I am coaching.” Continue reading...

items.title: Klopp, Alonso, Guardiola: Predicting what happens to Europe's top managers this summer
items.summary: With the Real Madrid and Manchester United jobs up for grabs, one thing is for certain this summer: The world's best managers will be in high demand.

items.title: Eddie Howe insists he would quit if he did not believe he was right man for Newcastle
items.summary: Manager has ‘no doubt’ he remains best person for job Newcastle travel to Spurs on Tuesday in 12th place Eddie Howe has said he would offer Newcastle his resignation if he believed he was no longer the right man to lead the presently struggling club. Howe’s team visit Tottenham on Tuesday night aiming to end the run of three straight Premier League defeats that has prompted their slide to 12th place. Victory would be their first in an away match since December but the 48-year-old manager remains confident he can navigate his fatigued side out of their slump. Continue reading...

items.title: I'd resign if I wasn't still right for job - Howe
items.summary: Eddie Howe says there is "no doubt in my mind" he is still the right manager for Newcastle despite one win in eight games.

items.title: WSL talking points: Arsenal punish City and Chelsea get into the groove
items.summary: Arsenal make the leaders pay, Sonia Bompastor is defiant and Manchester United’s squad is working in harmony Andrée Jeglertz said Manchester City’s “decision-making wasn’t ideal all the time during the game” in their 1-0 loss to Arsenal at the Emirates stadium on Sunday. He’s right. City may have had 22 touches in the opposition box to Arsenal’s 19 but they had only had one shot on target to Arsenal’s four. To some extent though, they have a hall pass for that lack of solid decision-making because it’s just so rare. Despite the defeat, City are sitting pretty at the top of the WSL table, their lead still a hefty eight points ahead of Manchester United. Should Arsenal win their game in hand, City’s lead will still be seven points. In a 12-team league and 22-game season, it’s incredibly unlikely that that gap will be bridged. Their goal difference is also 10 better than United’s. This is City’s title to lose and with the talent they have at their disposal the likelihood of any rot setting in is extremely slim. They play bottom-placed Leicester next, then struggling Aston Villa, who suffered a third back-to-back defeat, and those teams should fear City’s frustration. Suzanne Wrack Continue reading...

items.title: Ref Watch: Szoboszlai red, Guehi challenge and Newcastle handball analysed
